worth_it_explanation: DoubleVerify (DV) operates at two points in the programmatic buying cycle: pre-bid, where avoidance segments prevent the demand-side platform (DSP) bidder from competing for impressions that fail the brand's suitability criteria before the bid is placed, and post-bid, where measurement tags classify what was actually bought and report on it after delivery. Accounts that have DV integrated but have not activated pre-bid avoidance segments are paying for unsuitable impressions they could have avoided. Each check in this section is a configuration audit inside DV Pinnacle and the connected DSPs.
intro: DV's pre-bid suitability product works by supplying avoidance segment identifiers to DSP line items, insertion orders, or placements before the auction. The DSP consults those segments at bid time and declines to compete for any impression that falls within the avoidance criteria. This is distinct from DV's post-bid measurement tags, which fire on delivered impressions and generate reporting after the spend has already occurred. These two modes are not interchangeable. An account can run both simultaneously, but the pre-bid segment must be separately activated in each DSP. These checks confirm that pre-bid avoidance is active, that its category and risk-tier selection matches the post-bid monitoring profile, and that avoidance is applied consistently across the account's buying lines.

audit_checks.title: Pre-bid DV suitability segments are not applied on DSP line items
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When pre-bid avoidance segments are absent from DSP buying lines, the account relies entirely on post-bid measurement. Every impression that DV later flags as brand-unsafe, fraudulent, or unsuitable was already bought and billed before the classification happened. Pre-bid avoidance stops the bid before it enters the auction, so the spend never reaches those impressions. An account without pre-bid avoidance is measuring harm rather than preventing it.

educate.base:
DoubleVerify (DV) provides two operationally distinct products that are often confused because both appear in DV Pinnacle reporting. The first is a post-bid measurement tag: a JavaScript or pixel tag that fires on delivered impressions, classifies the surrounding content, and reports fraud, viewability, and suitability findings after delivery. The second is a pre-bid avoidance segment: an audience exclusion identifier that DSPs consult at auction time, before placing a bid, to determine whether a given impression meets the brand's suitability criteria.

The practical difference is timing. Post-bid tags observe what the DSP bought; pre-bid segments influence what the DSP bids on. Billing happens at the point of delivery, so a post-bid tag that flags an impression as brand-unsafe records an expense that has already occurred. A pre-bid segment that excludes the same impression class prevents the bid from entering those auctions entirely.

Activating pre-bid avoidance requires a separate step inside each DSP. In most DSPs the segment appears as a brand safety exclusion, third-party segment, or Authentic Brand Suitability identifier that is attached to the line item, ad set, or placement directly. A DV Pinnacle account that has post-bid measurement running does not automatically enable pre-bid avoidance. The two are set up independently.

A few signs suggest pre-bid avoidance may not be active on a given buying line:
  • DV Pinnacle reporting shows post-bid brand-safety or fraud flags on delivered impressions, but the corresponding DSP line item has no DV segment listed in its brand-safety or targeting exclusions
  • The DSP line item's brand-safety section references only a DV tag rather than a DV segment identifier
  • The DV Pinnacle account shows an Authentic Brand Suitability segment configured but with no DSP activation recorded against it
You may want to confirm the DSP-side configuration directly in each platform rather than relying on DV Pinnacle reporting alone. Pinnacle shows measurement data regardless of whether pre-bid avoidance is active.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser running display and video across multiple DSPs, fraudulent or brand-unsafe impressions inflate delivery counts without producing any shopping signal. Pre-bid avoidance keeps those impressions out of the auction entirely, so the viewability and engagement data that feeds line-item optimization reflects real users on real placements.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, unsuitable placements carry reputational risk beyond the wasted spend. A form submission captured alongside harmful content reflects on the brand. Pre-bid avoidance removes those placements before the bid rather than surfacing them in a post-campaign report.

learn_more.source_title: Enabling Pre-Bid DoubleVerify Segments in an Ad Set
Brand safety and media quality in Adobe Advertising DSP

educate.learn_more_note: DoubleVerify Pinnacle's configuration guides for pre-bid segment setup and activation are available within the DV Pinnacle platform (login required). Contact your DV account team for access to Pinnacle setup documentation.

investigate.intro: Confirm, for each active DSP buying line, whether a DV pre-bid avoidance segment is attached as a brand-safety exclusion or targeting exclusion, or whether the DV integration is present only as a post-bid measurement tag.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the Programmatic section of your account.

Locate the Authentic Brand Suitability or pre-bid segment configuration area. Confirm whether an Authentic Brand Suitability segment has been created and whether DSP activations are recorded against it.
Note which DSPs show active pre-bid segment activation. Any DSP listed as 'measurement only' or with no activation record is relying solely on post-bid measurement.

dsp_ui.label: DSP platform (each active buying DSP)
steps: Open the DSP and navigate to the line item, ad set, or placement you want to check.

Locate the brand-safety or third-party targeting section. Confirm whether a DV segment identifier appears there as an exclusion. A DV tag in the tracking or pixel section is not the same as a DV pre-bid segment in the targeting section.
If no DV segment identifier appears in targeting, the line item is using post-bid measurement only.
Repeat for each active buying line across each connected DSP. Lines within the same campaign may have different configurations if they were set up at different times.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active buying line and per connected DSP, whether a DV pre-bid avoidance segment is attached in the line item's targeting. A buying line with a DV pre-bid segment actively applied as an exclusion passes. A buying line with only a DV measurement tag and no pre-bid segment in targeting is a fail, recorded with the DSP name, line item or ad-set name, and the DV Pinnacle segment that should be activated there.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the targeting configuration for each active buying line, per DSP, above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that DV pre-bid suitability segments are not applied to {{site_name}}'s DSP buying lines. The DV integration is present as a post-bid measurement tag, which records brand-unsafe, fraudulent, and unsuitable impressions after delivery, but the pre-bid avoidance segment that would prevent the DSP from bidding on those impressions has not been activated in the line-item targeting. Every impression DV flags in post-bid reporting represents spend that was already billed. {{voice_recommend}} activating DV Authentic Brand Suitability segments as targeting exclusions in each connected DSP, confirming the segment identifier appears in the line item's brand-safety or targeting section rather than only in its tracking configuration, and verifying the activation in DV Pinnacle so pre-bid avoidance and post-bid measurement are running in parallel.

audit_checks.title: Pre-bid segment category selection does not match the post-bid monitoring profile
audit_checks.impact_rationale: When the categories and risk tiers selected in a pre-bid avoidance segment differ from those applied in post-bid monitoring, the two products measure different things and the results are not comparable. The account either avoids content it is not measuring, or measures content it is not avoiding, producing a reporting picture that does not reflect actual suitability exposure. Aligning the two profiles is the prerequisite for understanding whether pre-bid avoidance is working as intended.

educate.base:
DV's pre-bid avoidance and post-bid measurement each require a category and risk-tier configuration. Brand safety categories might include content classes such as adult content, hate speech, violence, or controversial news. Each category can be set to a floor level (blocking only the most severe content) or a stricter tier (blocking a broader range that includes moderately sensitive content). Fraud avoidance similarly distinguishes between general invalid traffic (GIVT) and sophisticated invalid traffic (SIVT), with different risk-tolerance settings available for each.

An account that activates pre-bid avoidance after post-bid measurement has already been running sometimes ends up with different category configurations in the two products. This happens because the pre-bid segment was configured separately, by a different team member, or at a different time, without consulting the post-bid monitoring settings. The result is a mismatch: the pre-bid segment avoids content in Category A but does not avoid Category B, while the post-bid monitor covers both. The post-bid report then surfaces Category B exposures that the pre-bid segment was never configured to prevent.

A few signs suggest a profile mismatch may exist:
  • DV Pinnacle post-bid reports show suitability flags in content categories that the pre-bid segment configuration does not list as exclusions
  • The pre-bid segment was created by copying a default template rather than by replicating the post-bid monitor's category list
  • Block rates dropped after pre-bid activation but post-bid flags for specific categories remain elevated, suggesting those categories are measured but not avoided
You may want to pull both the pre-bid segment configuration and the post-bid monitoring settings from DV Pinnacle side by side and compare the category and tier selections before drawing conclusions from the Pinnacle dashboard.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser, a category included in post-bid monitoring but absent from the pre-bid segment means the brand is paying for impressions next to that content type while also generating a report that flags the exposure. Aligning the two profiles so the pre-bid segment matches the post-bid monitor removes the category from the spend before it reaches delivery.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, a mismatch between pre-bid and post-bid profiles is especially worth examining for sensitive content categories such as political content or controversial health topics, where the brand's adjacency standards may differ from the DSP's default. A post-bid flag in a category the pre-bid segment does not cover represents a gap the brand may not have intended.

learn_more.source_title: Brand safety and media quality in Adobe Advertising DSP

educate.learn_more_note: DV Pinnacle's segment configuration interface, where pre-bid and post-bid category selections can be compared side by side, requires a DV Pinnacle login. Contact your DV account team for access to the segment management documentation.

investigate.intro: Pull the pre-bid segment category configuration and the post-bid monitoring profile from DV Pinnacle and compare each category and risk-tier selection side by side.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the segment management or Authentic Brand Suitability configuration area.

Open the pre-bid avoidance segment and record each category selected and the risk tier applied (for example, brand safety floor, standard suitability, or a custom tier; GIVT only or GIVT plus SIVT for fraud).
Navigate to the post-bid monitoring settings for the same advertiser. Record the same category and tier information.

Compare the two lists. Note any category that appears in post-bid monitoring but is absent from the pre-bid segment configuration, and any category in the pre-bid segment that is not covered by post-bid monitoring.

Flag mismatches for each category: a category monitored post-bid but not excluded pre-bid means spend is reaching that content; a category excluded pre-bid but not monitored post-bid means there is no reporting to confirm the avoidance is working.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per category and per risk tier, whether the pre-bid avoidance segment matches the post-bid monitoring profile. A category present in both profiles at the same risk tier passes. A category present in post-bid monitoring but absent from the pre-bid segment is a fail: the account is measuring exposure to that content but not preventing it. A category present in the pre-bid segment but absent from post-bid monitoring is a note: avoidance cannot be validated without measurement in that category.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the pre-bid and post-bid category and tier selections above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that {{site_name}}'s DV pre-bid avoidance segment and post-bid monitoring profile are not aligned across all categories. Specifically, categories flagged in post-bid DV reporting are absent from the pre-bid segment configuration, which means the account is measuring exposure to that content but not preventing bids from entering those auctions. This produces a reporting picture that overstates suitability exposure relative to what the pre-bid setup is actually blocking. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing both configurations in DV Pinnacle side by side, updating the pre-bid segment to include every category and risk tier present in the post-bid monitoring profile, and reconfirming the segment activation in each connected DSP after the update so the two products reflect the same brand suitability standard.

audit_checks.title: Pre-bid avoidance is applied unevenly across campaigns or DSPs, leaving some buying lines unprotected
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A pre-bid segment applied to some buying lines but not others creates unequal suitability protection within the same account. Line items without pre-bid avoidance may be spending against the same content classes the brand has chosen to exclude elsewhere, and those impressions are not visible as a gap in DV Pinnacle's aggregate reporting unless the practitioner segments the data by line item or DSP. The exposure concentrates in the unprotected lines without appearing as a campaign-level anomaly.

educate.base:
Pre-bid avoidance segments must be attached to each DSP buying line individually. In most DSPs, the segment is applied at the line item, ad set, or placement level rather than at the account or campaign level. A campaign with five line items requires the DV segment to be set on all five; attaching it to three leaves two unprotected, and those two lines will bid on impressions the other three are avoiding.

Uneven coverage develops in predictable ways. A line item is duplicated from a template that predates the pre-bid segment setup, so the copy does not carry the segment. A new campaign is built by a team member who was not part of the original pre-bid activation. A DSP is added to the media plan after pre-bid avoidance was configured in the existing DSPs, and the new DSP is not included in the activation.

The DV Pinnacle dashboard aggregates data across all connected buying lines, so a high-volume protected line item can mask suitability exposure on a lower-volume unprotected one at the campaign-level view. The gaps only surface when the data is broken out by line item or by DSP. The aggregate view does not separate protected from unprotected lines.

A few signs suggest coverage gaps may exist:
  • Line items within the same campaign were created at different times or by different team members, and no standard checklist was used for DV segment attachment
  • Post-bid DV suitability rates vary materially between line items in the same campaign without a targeting or placement explanation
  • A DSP was added to the media plan within the past six months, and the DV account team was not notified of the new buying source
You may want to check each active line item's targeting settings individually rather than inferring coverage from campaign-level DV Pinnacle reporting. The aggregate view does not separate protected from unprotected lines.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For a retail advertiser running separate line items for prospecting and retargeting, a pre-bid segment applied only to the prospecting line leaves the retargeting line unprotected. Retargeting lines typically run on user-behavior signals rather than contextual brand-safety signals, which makes this gap less visible in campaign review. You may want to confirm DV segment attachment on both line-item types.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser running campaigns across more than one DSP, a pre-bid segment activated in the primary DSP but not in a secondary one means a portion of the budget is buying impressions without suitability controls. You may want to check segment activation in each connected DSP separately, not only in the DSP where the segment was first set up.

learn_more.source_title: Enabling Pre-Bid DoubleVerify Segments in an Ad Set
Brand safety and media quality in Adobe Advertising DSP

educate.learn_more_note: DV Pinnacle's programmatic activation view, which shows per-DSP and per-line-item pre-bid segment status, requires a DV Pinnacle login. Contact your DV account team for access to the activation monitoring documentation.

investigate.intro: Check each active buying line across every connected DSP to confirm whether the DV pre-bid segment is attached, and identify any line without it.

dv_pinnacle.label: DoubleVerify Pinnacle
steps: Log in to DV Pinnacle and navigate to the programmatic activation or segment management section.
Pull a report segmented by DSP and by line item or ad set. Identify which lines show an active pre-bid segment and which show only post-bid measurement.
Note any DSP where the DV segment is not listed as activated. This indicates all buying lines in that DSP are relying on post-bid measurement only.

dsp_ui.label: DSP platform (each active buying DSP)
steps: Open each connected DSP and navigate to the campaign or insertion order level.
Review each active line item's targeting or brand-safety section. Confirm whether a DV segment identifier appears as a targeting exclusion on every line, or only on some.

Record the line items where the DV segment is absent. Note whether those lines were created from a template, duplicated from a protected line, or added to the campaign after the initial segment activation.

Cross-reference the unprotected lines against the DV Pinnacle post-bid report for the same date range. A line without pre-bid avoidance will show suitability flags in the post-bid data that a protected line in the same campaign may not.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active buying line and per connected DSP, whether the DV pre-bid segment is attached. A line with the segment applied as a targeting exclusion passes. A line without the segment is a fail, recorded with the DSP name, line item or ad-set name, and the date the line was created or most recently modified. Lines that were duplicated from a template without segment attachment are noted separately, as that indicates a systemic gap in the account's line-item setup process.
evaluate.fallback_message: Record the segment attachment status for each active buying line, per DSP, above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that DV pre-bid avoidance segments are applied unevenly across {{site_name}}'s buying lines, with some line items or DSPs carrying the segment and others running without it. The unprotected lines are bidding on impressions the brand has chosen to exclude elsewhere in the account, and that exposure does not surface as a gap in campaign-level DV Pinnacle reporting. {{voice_recommend}} auditing each active line item's targeting settings in every connected DSP, attaching the DV pre-bid segment to any line where it is absent, and establishing a standard line-item setup checklist that includes DV segment attachment as a required step before any new line goes live.
